summary: Linked src/melee/ft/ftcolanim.c by switching it from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, making a small source-level matching adjustment in ftCo_800BFFD0, and updating GALE01 symbol metadata so local compiler-generated constants/strings/floats are scoped correctly. The PR primarily moves the fighter color animation unit into the matching build.
what_changed: configure.py changed melee/ft/ftcolanim.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...), enabling the unit to link as matching.
src/melee/ft/ftcolanim.c removed an apparently unnecessary include of ft/types.h.
ftCo_800BFFD0 was rewritten slightly: a local s32 temp_r7 initialized as arg1 - 0x7B was replaced by a long tmp and an in-place subtraction, arg1 -= (tmp = 0x7B), before passing arg1 to lb_800144C8.
config/GALE01/symbols.txt updated multiple data symbols to local scope, especially compiler-style @NNN string/float/double constants in .data, .sdata, and .sdata2.
symbols.txt renamed some ftCo address-named string symbols to compiler local labels, including ftCo_803C6AE8 to @248, ftCo_803C6B08 to @249, and ftCo_804D3C10 to @250.
symbols.txt added local section labels ...data.0 at .data:0x803BAB60 and ...bss.0 at .bss:0x80433318.
smart_moves: The source change in ftCo_800BFFD0 preserved behavior while nudging register/local-variable allocation toward the original codegen; using a dummy long assignment inside the subtraction is a compact matching tactic.
The PR paired the configure.py matching flip with symbol-scope cleanup, avoiding a build/link transition that would leave generated local constants exposed as globals.
Converting address-style ftCo_* names for apparent file-local string constants back to @NNN local labels better reflects compiler-emitted local data and reduces misleading global API surface.
Removing an unused include before linking helps keep the translation unit dependency surface minimal.
decomp_lessons: When converting a C file from NonMatching to Matching, update both the build object classification and the symbol map; matching source often requires local constant labels to have correct scope.
Small C expression-shape changes can affect register allocation enough to match, even when the semantic computation is unchanged.
Do not leave compiler-local literals as global ftCo_* symbols unless there is evidence they are referenced cross-TU; @NNN local labels are appropriate for local strings and constants.
Symbol scope corrections in .sdata2 for float/double constants may be required when linking a new matching object, not just source changes.
A matching PR can be small in C code but large in symbols.txt because linkability depends on precise section, label, scope, and object metadata.
naming_conventions: Use Object(Matching, "melee/ft/ftcolanim.c") in configure.py once the translation unit links and matches.
Compiler-generated local constants and strings are represented as @NNN labels with scope:local in symbols.txt.
Address-derived names such as ftCo_803C6AE8 or ftCo_804D3C10 should not be retained for local string constants when the evidence indicates compiler-local data.
Local section labels use ellipsis names such as ...data.0 and ...bss.0 with type:label scope:local.
Existing project-style function names remain address based where unresolved, such as ftCo_800BFFAC and ftCo_800BFFD0.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use in-place mutation of an argument variable instead of introducing a separate temp when that better matches original register flow.
Introduce a dummy long temporary in an expression, as in arg1 -= (tmp = 0x7B), to influence constant materialization/register allocation without changing logic.
Remove unused includes to reduce incidental differences and keep the matching unit clean.
Correct local/global symbol scope for string and floating-point constants before linking a formerly nonmatching object.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The PR had one issue comment according to counts, but no comment body was included in the provided excerpt, so no specific reviewer guidance can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that flip fighter files from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py.
Search for uses of dummy long temporaries or assignment inside arithmetic expressions for matching register allocation.
Audit remaining ftCo_* address-named symbols that may actually be local @NNN string or constant labels.
Compare other symbols.txt changes that convert @NNN labels from global to local scope during link PRs.
